headline: Israel's reservists, pillars of a nation growing accustomed to a protracted war
subhead: As part of the offensive against Iran, Israel has called up 100,000 soldiers. The impact is felt across society, but the country has grown used to these successive waves of mobilization.

headline: Iran names Khamenei's son as new supreme leader
subhead: US President Donald Trump has dismissed Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as a 'lightweight' and insisted again on Sunday that he should have a say in the new leader's appointment. 'If he doesn't get approval from us he's not going to last long,' Trump told ABC News ahead of the announcement.

headline: 'In the Middle East and elsewhere, the tedious demands of diplomacy are essential for achieving peace'
subhead: Though Donald Trump had just chaired the Board of Peace's first session in Washington, he refused to allow time for negotiations with Iran to come to fruition, triggering a conflict with an outcome that remains uncertain.

headline: Thousands take to the streets for women's rights: 'We won't give up,' says Gisèle Pelicot at Paris march
subhead: In cities around the world, protesters marched on Sunday to mark International Women's Day and, in some cases, denounce the war in the Middle East. In France, Gisèle Pelicot, a global symbol in the fight against sexual violence, led a march in Paris.

headline: Paris mayoral elections: What do the candidates propose?
subhead: Le Monde breaks down the platforms of the capital's six main candidates on safety, cleanliness, housing and the environment ahead of the French municipal elections on March 15 and 22.

What the Greek myth of Pandora reveals about the deep-seated fear of women
subhead: By opening a jar entrusted to her by the gods, Pandora becomes the instrument of their vengeance against rebellious humans and unleashes a torrent of plagues upon her fellow mortals. This misogynistic myth seems to contain all the resentments one half of humanity harbors against the other.

headline: Thomas Piketty: 'In the face of Trump's militaristic drift and looming disaster, Europe must equip itself to have global influence'
subhead: Following the intervention in Venezuela, the war against Iran launched by the US president signals yet another admission of weakness by the United States, observes the economist in his column. This military agitation primarily serves to bolster the US economy.

headline: France's Iranian community torn between republicans and monarchists
subhead: Many Iranians in France hope for regime change back home. However, those in favor of democracy are losing ground to supporters of a return to the monarchy, led by the son of Iran's former shah.
